title: ICE Agent Fatally Shoots Minneapolis Resident Renee Good
excerpt: ICE officer Jonathan Ross fatally shot 37-year-old U.S. citizen Renee Nicole Good through her car windshield during an enforcement operation in Minneapolis. Bystander video contradicted the DHS account that she posed an imminent threat, sparking national protests and calls for accountability.
content:
On January 7, 2026, ICE officer Jonathan Ross shot and killed 37-year-old Renée Nicole Good during an enforcement operation in a Minneapolis residential neighborhood. Good, a mother of three and U.S. citizen, was sitting in her vehicle when agents approached.

DHS Secretary Kristi Noem immediately defended the shooting as self-defense, claiming Good had attempted to run over officers. Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey called that account "garbage," stating bystander video showed Good's vehicle turning away from officers when she was shot. Minnesota officials later stated the footage did not support DHS's description of an imminent threat.

  • The shooting was the first fatal use of deadly force by ICE during Operation Metro Surge, the largest immigration enforcement operation ever conducted by DHS
  • The FBI was assigned to lead the investigation; the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension (BCA) was later excluded from joint investigation access by the U.S. Attorney's Office
  • Hennepin County Medical Examiner ruled Good's death a homicide
  • Congressional Democrats immediately demanded answers about ICE training and use-of-force policy
"Our officer followed his training, did exactly what he has been taught to do in that situation," — DHS Secretary Kristi Noem (claim disputed by video evidence and local officials)
mediaPlaceholder: Bystander video of the January 7 shooting on a Minneapolis street showing agents surrounding Good's vehicle
